2.gl: figuratively, a magistrate; by Hebraism, very
2.dv: of uncertain affinity; a deity, especially (with G3588 (ὁ)) the supreme Divinity;

2.gl: something said (including the thought); by implication, a topic (subject of discourse), also reasoning (the mental faculty) or motive; by extension, a computation; specially, (with the article in John) the Divine Expres
2.kj: account, cause, communication, X concerning, doctrine, fame, X have to do, intent, matter, mouth, preaching, question, reason, + reckon, remove, say(-ing), shew, X speaker, speech,

4.gl: supreme in authority, i.e. (as noun) controller; by implication, Master (as a respectful title)
a foreteller ("prophet"); by analogy, an inspired speaker; by extension, a poet
4.dv: from a compound of G4253 (πρό) and G5346 (φημί);

6.dv: of uncertain affinity; a deity, especially (with G3588 (ὁ)) the supreme Divinity;
feminine of a derivative of G4990 (σωτήρ) as (properly, abstract) noun;

6.gl: flesh (as stripped of the skin), i.e. (strictly) the meat of an animal (as food), or (by extension) the body (as opposed to the soul (or spirit), or as the symbol of what is external, or as the means of kindred), or (by
6.dv: probably from the base of G4563 (σαρόω);

8.dv: of uncertain affinity; a deity, especially (with G3588 (ὁ)) the supreme Divinity;

16.gl: sacred (physically, pure, morally blameless or religious, ceremonially, consecrated)
to immerse, submerge; to make whelmed (i.e. fully wet); used only (in the New Testament) of ceremonial ablution, especially (technically) of the ordinance of Christian baptism

21.gl: the sky; by extension, heaven (as the abode of God); by implication, happiness, power, eternity; specially, the Gospel (Christianity)
21.dv: perhaps from the same as G3735 (ὄρος) (through the idea of elevation);
